  • Ship constantly: Speed is our weapon. We release early, iterate rapidly, and value feedback over theoretical perfection.
  • We are all in customer success: Titles don’t matter here. Whether you’re in Finance, Engineering, or Sales, our success is defined by our customers’ success.
  • Seek diverse perspectives: We have team members in nearly 50 countries. We break down silos and believe that our collective responsibility leads to stronger decisions.
  • Roll up your sleeves: We are a flat organization where leaders are unafraid to be involved and stay connected to the details.
  • Help each other thrive: We lift each other up. Even under pressure, we act with intelligence and heart.
  • Default to transparency: We share information by default, and when we can't, we explain why. Transparency builds trust and simply enables better outcomes.
  • Make the work matter: We avoid activity for activity’s sake. We prioritize work that solves real problems.

Our mortal enemies

Being direct about who we are also means being clear about what we aren't. Along with our principles, we identified our mortal enemies, which are behaviors that poison the well and kill innovation.

We actively fight against bureaucracy, stagnation, finger-pointing, mediocrity, busywork, unproductive resistance, and standing on the sidelines. And, to put it bluntly, we have a "no assholes" policy, regardless of how high-performing someone might be.

The road ahead

These principles are designed to help us make independent decisions in a pinch. Whether it’s choosing to open-source a feature to build long-term trust (long-term greedy) or sharing difficult updates openly (default to transparency), this is how we operate.

We’re sharing these publicly because transparency matters to us, and because if you work with Grafana Labs, or want to join us, you should know exactly what you’re signing up for.

Let’s get to work.

Learn more about the Grafana Labs team and our remote-first culture. And to explore opportunities to become a future Grafanista, visit our careers page. We hope to hear from you!

】

Matt Toback 是 Grafana Labs 的文化副总裁。

我们早在 2020 年 12 月就发布了我们最初的公司价值观。我们是一家年轻的公司，发展迅速，而且完全处于远程办公状态。我们当时的价值观是有抱负的，并描绘了我们想要成为的公司类型。这些价值观确实发挥了作用，而且很重要。你可以在日常对话中听到它们的使用，它们帮助我们取得了今天的成就。 

但成长总会暴露差距。

随着时间的推移，我们意识到一些重要的事情：我们已经形成了一种非常真实、非常具体的工作方式，但这种方式并没有完全反映在我们写下的内容以及它如何帮助我们做出决策中。文化就在这里。决定就在这里。图案就在这里。但话语却有些滞后。实际上，我们运行的操作系统比我们内部 wiki 和外部职业页面上发布的操作系统更新。

因此我们决定对其进行升级。 

但更大的问题不是是否更新我们的价值观；而是是否更新我们的价值观。这是如何。在我们成长的这个阶段，什么真正能为我们服务？什么样的框架能够反映我们今天的身份并仍然推动我们前进？

为什么是指导原则？

当我们考虑更新我们的价值观时，我们持怀疑态度。正如我们的首席执行官 Raj Dutt 所说，企业价值观往往最终成为陈词滥调，就像你在办公室墙上看到的那些毫无意义的励志海报一样。

我们想要一套有点逆向思维的原则。这些想法可能并不适用于每家公司，但显然适合我们。我们想要一个可以每天使用的操作系统来应对挑战，扩展我们的文化，并在我们成长的过程中推动行动。

这些不仅仅是温暖和模糊的情感；它们是我们的使命。它们是成为 Grafanista 的基本真理。它们解释了我们对彼此的期望，并成为我们做出决策的基础，尤其是当事情变得复杂时。

Grafanista 指导原则

这十项原则定义了我们如何驾驭世界，也是我们赖以生存的准则：

  • 长期贪婪：这是我们的最爱。这意味着我们优先考虑持久价值而不是短期收益。如果这意味着为明天奠定更好的基础，我们愿意承受今天的打击，无论是收入还是安逸。
  • 灵魂完好无损：我们诚信经营。我们希望几十年后能够直视对方的眼睛，不仅尊重我们共同完成的工作，而且尊重我们完成这些工作的方式。
  • 做（并说）困难的事情：我们不会回避困难的事情。我们进行令人不舒服的对话并推动必要的改变，因为避免的成本总是更高。
  • 不断发货：速度是我们的武器。我们尽早发布、快速迭代，并且重视反馈而不是理论完美。
  • 我们都致力于客户的成功：这里没关系。无论您从事财务、工程还是销售工作，我们的成功都取决于客户的成功。
  • 寻求不同的观点：我们的团队成员遍布近 50 个国家/地区。我们打破孤岛，相信我们的集体责任会带来更强有力的决策。
  • 卷起袖子：我们是一个扁平化的组织，领导者不畏惧参与，并与细节保持联系。
  • 帮助彼此成长：我们互相激励。即使在压力下，我们也会以智慧和真心行事。
  • 默认透明度：我们默认共享信息，当我们不能共享信息时，我们会解释原因。透明度可以建立信任并带来更好的结果。
  • 让工作变得重要：我们为了活动而避免活动。我们优先考虑解决实际问题的工作。

我们的死敌

直接了解我们是谁也意味着清楚我们不是什么。在坚持原则的同时，我们也明确了我们的死敌，那就是毒害水井、扼杀创新的行为。

我们积极反对官僚主义、停滞不前、相互指责、平庸、忙碌、无效抵抗、袖手旁观。而且，坦率地说，我们有“禁止混蛋”政策，无论某人的表现有多出色。

前进的道路

这些原则旨在帮助我们在紧要关头做出独立决策。无论是选择开源一项功能来建立长期信任（长期贪婪），还是公开分享困难的更新（默认透明），这都是我们的运作方式。

我们公开分享这些是因为透明度对我们很重要，而且如果您与 Grafana Labs 合作，或者想加入我们，您应该确切地知道您要注册什么。

让我们开始工作吧。

了解更多关于 Grafana Labs团队 和 我们的 远程优先文化。 要探索成为未来 Grafanista 的机会，请访问我们的 职业页。 我们希望收到您的来信！

item.title: 【Grafana 12.4 release: faster and easier data visualization, observability as code updates, and more】Grafana 12.4 版本：更快、更轻松的数据可视化、代码更新的可观察性等等
item.description:
【

As we gear up for Grafana 13, the next major release of the open source data visualization platform that we’ll announce at GrafanaCON this April, our engineering team is still shipping some powerful new features along the way.

Case in point: Grafana 12.4 is officially here, and there’s a lot to be excited about. 

The latest minor release includes a ton of updates that help you build and design dashboards faster than ever, as well as manage and scale those dashboards seamlessly over time.

Below are just some of the highlights from Grafana 12.4. If you want to explore all the latest updates, please refer to the changelog or our What’s New documentation, and be sure to check out the TL;DR video below.

Managing dashboards as code: the latest in Git Sync 

Available in public preview in Grafana Cloud, and experimental in Grafana OSS and Grafana Enterprise 

As you scale your Grafana instance, managing dashboards through the UI alone can become chaotic — changes are hard to track, duplication increases, and consistency suffers. While managing dashboards as code can improve visibility and help with version control, it often adds complexity and creates bottlenecks because of custom CI and processes.

Git Sync, a Grafana feature we rolled out last year, bridges that gap by bringing a native GitOps workflow into Grafana. This enables teams to version dashboards, submit pull requests, and manage approvals seamlessly within GitHub or Grafana directly.

Git Sync is now available in public preview in Grafana Cloud and experimental in Grafana Enterprise and OSS. It includes new features that give teams even more flexibility and control when managing dashboards as code: 

  • Github App authentication: You can now connect Grafana to your repositories using a personal access token or with GitHub Apps for enhanced security.
  • Enhanced branch selection in Save drawers: Branch selection now feels more natural and aligns with the experience you’re used to when submitting changes to provisioned resources.

To learn more, please check out our Git Sync blog post and documentation. 

Building intuitive, easier-to-navigate dashboards: what’s new in Dynamic dashboards 

Available in public preview in all editions of Grafana

As teams connect more data sources to Grafana, their dashboards become larger and more complex. This can lead to cluttered layouts, empty panels, and excessive scrolling, making dashboards harder to navigate and maintain. 

Last year, we introduced Dynamic dashboards to address this very challenge. Based on the robust foundation of the Scenes library and our updated dashboard schema, Dynamic dashboards delivers a more intuitive, responsive, and scalable experience to better support growing teams.

Now in public preview, Dynamic dashboards includes new features and a revamped user experience that make it even easier to find the exact insights you need, when you need them. 

Flexible tabs

You can now structure your dashboards horizontally, as well as vertically, to organize content for different contexts and user groups.

Show/hide functionality

Show or hide panels, rows, or tabs based on the selected variable or whether the panel returns data, so viewers only see what’s relevant to their use case or context.

Auto grid layout

By setting your row, tab, or dashboard to Auto grid, the panel size will adapt to the space available in the display. You can configure minimum column width, row height, and the maximum number of columns per row to meet your requirements. 

If you prefer setting different panel widths and heights within the same row, you can use the Custom layout option, which is the same as the traditional dashboards grid. Auto grid also enables the show/hide functionality. (To avoid leaving gaps in the dashboard, you must set the layout to auto grid before configuring any show/hide rules.)

Dashboard content outline

You can now quickly discover the content you need in your dashboards through a clickable outline. 

As an editor, click on an element in the outline to scroll to it and select it to make quick changes to the title or description. As a viewer, click on a panel, row, or tab to navigate to it instantly and find the information you’re looking for.

Side toolbar instead of a second top toolbar

To provide additional vertical space, we’ve replaced the second top toolbar with a toolbar on the right side of the screen. This helps both viewers and editors see more information at once. You can find the dashboard content outline and alert rules in the side toolbar, and editors can also access dashboard settings there.

Context-aware editing from the dashboard

Quickly update a panel’s title and description from the dashboard sidebar without entering panel edit mode. This sidebar also lets you quickly edit rows, tabs, and template variables.

To learn more about Dynamic dashboards, please check out our documentation. 

Visualizing data faster: Suggested dashboards, templates, and more

Grafana 12.4 includes a host of new features that help you go from zero to insights in minutes, regardless of experience level. 

Suggested dashboards tailored to your data sources 

Available in public preview in all editions of Grafana 

For many users, especially those new to Grafana, opening a blank dashboard can feel a bit overwhelming. 

That’s why we’re introducing Suggested dashboards, a feature that surfaces suggestions for pre-built dashboards based on your connected data sources. Curated from both Grafana Labs and the broader OSS community, these pre-built dashboards help you:

  • Enable contextual discovery: Get suggestions for pre-built dashboards that are tied specifically to the data source you have connected.
  • Accelerate setup: Immediately load and save suggested dashboards, which you can use as a starting point for further customization.
  • Reduce friction: Streamline the onboarding process by offering actionable content directly after data source configuration.

Here’s how it works:

After you successfully configure a data source, click the Build a dashboard button.

The empty dashboard that opens up will automatically display a list of suggested dashboards based on the data source.

To learn more, please visit our docs.

Creating dashboards from templates 

Available in public preview in all editions of Grafana

You can now create dashboards based on templates using sample data, and then connect your actual data sets to power them with real metrics. This provides a structured, best-practice starting point for your dashboard layouts. 

Templates provide standardized dashboard layouts designed to help you answer both engineering or business questions. For example, the DORA template allows all teams within an organization to measure delivery performance using a widely adopted industry framework.

All templates include a banner panel that clearly indicates the dashboard is using sample data:

To make the dashboard your own, save it, update each panel’s data source to connect to your own data, and make any other changes to display your data effectively. You can also use Grafana Assistant, the AI-powered agent in Grafana Cloud, to update the dashboard with your own data sources and create queries for you.

When you’ve finished making your changes, remove the banner panel:

You can access this feature from the New drop-down menu in dashboards, the quick Add button in the top-right corner anywhere in Grafana, as well as in the command palette.

To learn more, visit our docs. 

Updated Visualization suggestions 

Available in public preview in all editions of Grafana 

Initially rolled out in Grafana 8.3, Visualization suggestions have been updated to provide more applicable suggestions, and are now the default method to select a panel visualization.

When you preview a visualization using the Suggestions feature, you now get a full-sized preview of the suggestion in the panel editor before you select it.

Alongside some UI enhancements, we’ve also improved the quality of suggestions by leveraging metadata from your data sources, such as data frame types in the data plane, to recommend visualization types best suited to your data.

To try out the updated visualization suggestions experience, enable the newVizSuggestions feature toggle.

More updates to streamline data visualization 

Reducing visual noise with the new dashboard controls menu 

Generally available in all editions of Grafana

Variables taking over your screen? You can now choose to hide variables, annotation controls, or dashboard links from the main toolbar and place them in the new dashboard controls menu instead.

When any of these items are configured to appear in the menu, Grafana shows a dedicated toolbar button with a count of how many controls are available. Clicking the button opens a neatly structured menu where variables appear first, followed by annotation controls and links, each grouped for easy navigation.

Filtering query variable values with regex patterns

Generally available in all editions of Grafana

You can now apply a regex pattern to the display text instead of the variable value  to find the right query variable values to show. This is a great shortcut when the text you see in the UI doesn’t match the raw value you need to capture.

For example, when you configure query variables with regex for data sources like Azure Monitor, Amazon CloudWatch, and AWS IoT, the regex matches the value. However, this value is often different from the display text, which makes it difficult to match the value you need.

To fix this mismatch, use the Apply regex to option in your query variable settings to select whether the regex applies to the value or the display text.

To learn more, refer to the variable documentation.

Mapping one variable to multiple values

Generally available in all editions of Grafana

We’re excited to introduce multi-property variables — a simple, powerful way to map several identifiers to a single variable.

Instead of creating and keeping multiple variables for the same logical concept in sync (for example, an environment identified as “dev” vs. “development”), you can map all of those identifiers to one variable and then reference any property you need in panels and queries. This reduces dashboard clutter and makes multi-source dashboards much easier to author and maintain.

This feature is supported for the Custom and Query variable types. For query variables, you can create multi-property variables for Infinity and PostgreSQL data sources.

You can read more about this feature and how it works in our documentation.

Revamped gauge visualization

Available in public preview in all editions of Grafana

We’ve reimagined the gauge visualization to provide more data representation options based on your needs.

For example, the circular shape has been introduced as an alternative to the existing Arc gauge under the new Style option.

We’ve also added sparkline support to the gauge, which improves its usefulness as an alternative to the stat visualization for time series data.

We added gradient support that does much more than look pretty; it helps users interpret panels with thresholds, or with series that use a continuous color scheme.

The new gauge visualization also features accessibility improvements. The Center glow option renders the gauge’s current color value as a transparent glow behind the gauge’s text. This replaces the previous behavior of changing the color of the text itself, and helps avoid situations where text can become illegible due to an individual user’s theme.

Lastly, there are several other new configuration options, including:

  • Rounded bars (under Bar style)
  • Configurable bar thickness
  • Endpoint markers for readability
  • New text display modes to precisely configure the information presented in your visualization

To try out the revamped gauge visualization, enable the newGauge feature toggle. And to learn more about this visualization type, in general, please check out our docs.

Time range pan and zoom

Generally available in all editions of Grafana

We’re excited to introduce time range pan and zoom in Grafana dashboards and panels, a new way to explore your metrics and data more intuitively and efficiently. 

You can use dashboard-level controls to jump one full time span backward or forward. For more precise time travel, we’ve made the x-axis of time series visualizations interactive at the panel level, as well. Simply click and drag the x-axis timestamps horizontally to move forward or backward in time, then release to update the dashboard time range.

To zoom in on a time range, click and drag in the visualization area from a start time to any end time, and then release. To zoom out, double-click on the visualization area to expand the time range by half on either side of the center, showing twice as much time.

Finally, two new time range keyboard shortcuts have been added to Grafana. Typing t+ zooms in to half the current time range, and conversely t- zooms out to twice the current time range.

Time range pan and zoom applies to all visualizations that support time series data:

  • Candlestick
  • Heatmap
  • State timeline
  • Status history
  • Time series

More efficient and flexible log exploration 

Grafana 12.4 provides new, more customizable options for exploring your logging data, especially for teams using OpenTelemetry or the Grafana Logs Drilldown queryless app.

Enhanced display for OpenTelemetry log lines

Experimental in all editions of Grafana

Grafana now provides smarter defaults for browsing OpenTelemetry log lines, optimized for the way OTel structures logging data.

OpenTelemetry log producers send less information in the log body and more data as structured metadata, or key–value pairs for resource attributes, log fields, and log attributes. Previously, much of this information was only visible when expanding a log line, making important context easy to miss.

Now, Grafana detects OTel log lines and surfaces relevant metadata directly in the log view. Overall, this change makes OpenTelemetry logs easier to scan, faster to understand, and more useful by default, especially in high-cardinality or highly structured logging environments. 

For more information, reference our What’s New page. 

Logs Drilldown updates

Available in public preview in all editions of Grafana 

With Grafana Logs Drilldown, you can visualize and explore your logs without having to write LogQL queries. Part of our suite of Grafana Drilldown apps for queryless, point-and-click data exploration, Logs Drilldown now allows you to:

  • Configure default columns: You can now configure default columns for Logs Drilldown directly from the plugin configuration page. This lets administrators define which fields (or columns) are displayed by default based on the selected set of labels, ensuring users immediately see the most relevant information for their use case.
  • Save and resume log explorations: You can now save a log exploration in Logs Drilldown and return to it later. Saved explorations include your active filters, so you don’t have to recreate the same view each time you investigate an issue.

Please check out our technical docs to learn more about Logs Drilldown.

Identity and access management enhancements

Here’s a look at some of the latest features to help you secure your Grafana environment and manage user access. 

SCIM user and team provisioning

Generally available in Grafana Cloud and Grafana Enterprise

You can now automate your entire user lifecycle and team structures directly from your identity provider (IdP). 

We introduced SCIM in public preview last year, and with the move to general availability as part of Grafana 12.4, we’ve removed the “first login” bottleneck. This allows centralized operators to provision, configure, and secure users before they even open the app.

Instead of managing complex sync pipelines and waiting for users to log in to assign permissions, you can now build, test, and scale your entire engineering organizational structure in minutes. Key benefits include:

  • Onboard instantly: Users and teams are created the moment they are added to your IdP, enabling immediate configuration in tools like IRM.
  • Automate deprovisioning: Gain peace of mind with automatic seat reclamation and instant access removal the second a user is deactivated in your IdP.
  • Scale faster: Early adopters of SCIM see more active users within 90 days compared to SAML-only customers, because removing manual friction accelerates platform adoption.

To learn more, please check out our docs and this blog post. 

RBAC for saved queries 

Available in public preview in Grafana Cloud and Grafana Enterprise 

Saved queries — a feature in Grafana that makes it easy to save, share, and reuse queries — now supports role-based access control (RBAC), giving teams a clearer and safer way to share and manage queries.

Two custom roles are available:

  • Writer: Create, update, and delete saved queries
  • Reader: Reuse existing saved queries

This replaces the previous per-query sharing checkbox with consistent, role-based permissions, making it easier to collaborate on saved queries without risking accidental changes.

To learn more, check out our saved queries documentation.

Extending Grafana: data source updates

We believe you should be able to query and visualize your data, regardless of where that data lives. As a result, we’re always working to expand and enhance our line-up of Grafana data sources. 

Here’s a look at the latest updates.

New release of the Zabbix data source for Grafana 

Generally available in all editions of Grafana 

Version 6.1 of the Zabbix data source for Grafana is here, providing broader sharing capabilities and improved stability for a smoother monitoring experience.

Key updates include:

  • Sharing dashboards with anyone: You can now share your Grafana dashboards for Zabbix externally without requiring recipients to have access to your Grafana organization. 
  • Enhanced stability and performance: New query guardrails provide proactive warnings when a query might exceed Zabbix API limits, helping you prevent performance degradation and unexpected resource usage. You can also explicitly set query timeouts in the configuration, preventing long-running queries from impacting your system.
  • Granular filtering with host tags: You can now use Zabbix host tags directly in your Grafana Zabbix queries, enabling more granular, flexible data filtering and visualization.

You can read more about Zabbix data source in our docs. 

Default spreadsheets in the Google Sheets data source

Generally available in all editions of Grafana 

You can now set a default spreadsheet when creating a Google Sheets data source, so it’s automatically available whenever the data source is used. This means instead of repeatedly selecting the same file, you can define it once and reuse it across dashboards, panels, and queries.

The result is a smoother workflow for common use cases like tracking KPIs in shared spreadsheets, visualizing operational checklists, or building internal dashboards from collaboratively maintained data. 

To learn more about the Google Sheets data source for Grafana, please visit our docs. 

Learn more about Grafana

For an in-depth list of all the new features in Grafana, check out our Grafana documentation, the Grafana changelog, or our What’s New documentation.

Join the Grafana Labs community

We invite you to engage with the Grafana Labs community forums. Share your experiences with the new features, discuss best practices, and explore creative ways to integrate these updates into your workflows. Your insights and use cases are invaluable in enriching the Grafana ecosystem.

Upgrade to Grafana 12.4

Download Grafana 12.4 today or experience all the latest features by signing up for Grafana Cloud, which offers an actually useful forever-free tier and plans for every use case. Sign up for a free Grafana Cloud account today.

Our Grafana upgrade guide also provides step-by-step instructions for those looking to upgrade from an earlier version to ensure a smooth transition.

A special thanks to our community

We extend our heartfelt gratitude to the Grafana community!

Your contributions, ranging from pull requests to valuable feedback, are crucial in continually enhancing Grafana. And your enthusiasm and dedication inspire us at Grafana Labs to persistently innovate and elevate the Grafana platform.

当我们为 Grafana 13 做好准备时，我们将在今年 4 月的 GrafanaCON 上宣布开源数据可视化平台的下一个主要版本 Grafana 13，我们的工程团队仍在不断推出一些强大的新功能。

举个例子：Grafana 12.4 正式发布这里，有很多值得兴奋的事情。 

最新的次要版本包含大量更新，可帮助您比以往更快地构建和设计仪表板，以及随着时间的推移无缝管理和扩展这些仪表板。

以下只是 Grafana 12.4 的一些亮点。如果您想了解所有最新更新，请参阅变更日志或我们的新增内容文档，并务必查看下面的 TL;DR 视频。

以代码方式管理仪表板：Git Sync 的最新内容

在 Grafana 的公共预览版中提供云、Grafana OSS 和 Grafana Enterprise 中的实验

当您扩展 Grafana 实例时，仅通过 UI 管理仪表板可能会变得混乱 - 更改难以跟踪、重复增加且一致性受到影响。虽然将仪表板作为代码进行管理可以提高可见性并有助于版本控制，但由于自定义 CI 和流程，它通常会增加复杂性并产生瓶颈。

Git Sync 是我们去年推出的 Grafana 功能，它通过将本机 GitOps 工作流程引入 Grafana 来弥补这一差距。这使团队能够直接在 GitHub 或 Grafana 中对仪表板进行版本控制、提交拉取请求以及无缝管理审批。

Git Sync 现已在 Grafana Cloud 中提供公共预览版，并在 Grafana Enterprise 和 OSS 中进行实验。它包含的新功能可让团队在以代码形式管理仪表板时获得更大的灵活性和控制力：

  • Github 应用程序身份验证：您现在可以使用个人访问令牌或使用 GitHub 应用程序将 Grafana 连接到您的存储库以增强安全性。
  • 在“保存抽屉”中增强分支选择：现在，分支选择感觉更加自然，并且符合您在向已配置资源提交更改时所习惯的体验。

要了解更多信息，请查看我们的 Git Sync 博客文章 和 文档。 

构建直观、更易于导航的仪表板：动态仪表板的新增功能

在所有版本的 Grafana 中提供公共预览

随着团队将更多数据源连接到 Grafana，他们的仪表板变得更大、更复杂。这可能会导致布局混乱、面板空白和过度滚动，从而使仪表板更难以导航和维护。 

去年，我们推出了动态仪表板来应对这一挑战。基于场景库的强大基础和我们更新的仪表板架构，动态仪表板提供了更直观、响应更快且可扩展的体验，以更好地支持成长中的团队。

动态仪表板现已推出公共预览版包含新功能和改进的用户体验，使您可以在需要时更轻松地找到所需的准确见解。 

灵活的选项卡

您现在可以水平和垂直构建仪表板，以便为不同的上下文和用户组组织内容。

显示/隐藏功能

根据所选变量或面板是否返回数据显示或隐藏面板、行或选项卡，以便查看者只能看到与其用例或相关的内容

自动网格布局

通过将行、选项卡或仪表板设置为自动网格，面板大小将适应显示屏中的可用空间。您可以配置最小列宽、行高和每行最大列数以满足您的要求。 

如果您喜欢在同一行内设置不同的面板宽度和高度，可以使用自定义布局选项，该选项与传统仪表板网格相同。自动网格还启用显示/隐藏功能。 （为了避免在仪表板中留下间隙，您必须在配置任何显示/隐藏规则之前将布局设置为自动网格。）

仪表板内容大纲

您现在可以通过可点击的大纲快速发现仪表板中所需的内容。 

作为编辑者，单击大纲中的某个元素可滚动到该元素并选择它以快速更改标题或说明。作为查看者，单击面板、行或选项卡即可立即导航到它并查找您要查找的信息。

侧面工具栏而不是第二个顶部工具栏

为了提供额外的垂直空间，我们将第二个顶部工具栏替换为屏幕右侧的工具栏。这有助于观众和编辑同时看到更多信息。您可以在侧边工具栏中找到仪表板内容大纲和警报规则，编辑者还可以在此处访问仪表板设置。

从仪表板进行上下文感知编辑

从仪表板侧边栏快速更新面板的标题和说明，而无需进入面板编辑模式。此侧边栏还可以让您快速编辑行、选项卡和模板变量。

要了解有关动态仪表板的更多信息，请查看我们的文档。 

更快地可视化数据：建议的仪表板、模板等

Grafana 12.4 包含大量新功能，可帮助您在几分钟内从零获得洞察，无论经验水平如何。 

根据您的数据源量身定制的建议仪表板

在所有版本的 Grafana 中都提供公开预览版

对于许多用户，尤其是 Grafana 的新用户来说，打开空白仪表板可能会感到有点不知所措。 

因此，我们推出建议仪表板，该功能可根据您连接的数据源显示预构建仪表板的建议。这些预构建的仪表板由 Grafana Labs 和更广泛的 OSS 社区精心策划，可帮助您：

  • 启用上下文发现：获取专门与您连接的数据源关联的预构建仪表板的建议。
  • 加速设置：立即加载并保存建议的仪表板，您可以将其用作进一步自定义的起点。
  • Reduce摩擦：通过在数据源配置后直接提供可操作的内容来简化入门流程。

其工作原理如下：

成功配置数据源后，单击构建仪表板按钮。

打开的空仪表板将自动显示基于数据源的建议仪表板列表。

要了解更多信息，请访问我们的文档。

从模板创建仪表板

在所有版本的公开预览版中提供Grafana

您现在可以使用示例数据基于模板创建仪表板，然后连接您的实际数据集，以通过真实指标为其提供支持。这为您的仪表板布局提供了结构化的最佳实践起点。 

模板提供标准化仪表板布局，旨在帮助您回答工程或业务问题。例如，DORA 模板允许组织内的所有团队使用广泛采用的行业框架来衡量交付绩效。

所有模板都包含一个横幅面板，清楚地指示仪表板正在使用示例数据：

要使仪表板成为您自己的仪表板，请保存它，更新每个面板的数据源o 连接到您自己的数据，并进行任何其他更改以有效地显示您的数据。您还可以使用 Grafana Cloud 中由 AI 驱动的代理 Grafana Assistant，使用您自己的数据源更新仪表板并为您创建查询。

完成更改后，删除横幅面板：

您可以访问此功能从仪表板中的新建下拉菜单、Grafana 右上角任意位置的快速添加按钮以及命令面板中。

要了解更多信息，请访问我们的文档。 

更新的可视化建议

在所有版本的 Grafana 中提供公开预览版

最初在 Grafana 中推出8.3，可视化建议已更新以提供更适用的建议，并且现在是选择面板可视化的默认方法。

当您使用“建议”功能预览可视化时，您现在会看到在选择建议之前，在面板编辑器中对建议进行全尺寸预览。

除了一些 UI 增强功能之外，我们还通过利用数据源中的元数据（例如数据平面中的数据框架类型）来提高建议的质量，以推荐最适合的可视化类型

要尝试更新的可视化建议体验，请启用 newVizSuggestions 功能开关。

更多简化数据可视化的更新

使用新的仪表板控件菜单减少视觉噪音

在所有版本的 Grafana 中普遍可用

变量接管您的屏幕？现在，您可以选择在主工具栏中隐藏变量、注释控件或仪表板链接，并将它们放置在新的仪表板控件菜单中

当这些项目中的任何一个配置为出现在菜单中时，Grafana 会显示一个专用的工具栏按钮，其中包含可用控件的计数。单击该按钮将打开一个结构整齐的菜单，其中首先出现变量，然后是注释控件和链接，每个都分组以便于导航。

使用正则表达式模式过滤查询变量值

通常可用在所有版本的 Grafana 中

您现在可以将正则表达式模式应用于显示文本而不是变量值，以找到正确的查询变量要显示的值。当您在 UI 中看到的文本与需要捕获的原始值不匹配时，这是一个很好的快捷方式。

例如，当您使用正则表达式为 Azure Monitor、Amazon CloudWatch 和 AWS IoT 等数据源配置查询变量时，正则表达式会与值匹配。但是，此值通常与显示文本不同，这使得很难匹配您需要的值。

要解决此不匹配问题，请使用查询变量设置中的将正则表达式应用于选项来选择正则表达式是应用于该值还是应用于显示文本。

要了解更多信息，请参阅变量文档。

将一个变量映射到多个值

通常在所有版本中提供Grafana

我们很高兴推出多属性变量 - 一种将多个标识符映射到单个变量的简单而强大的方法。

而不是创建和保留多个同步同一逻辑概念的变量（例如，标识为“dev”与“development”的环境），您可以将所有这些标识符映射到一个变量，然后在面板和查询中引用您需要的任何属性。这减少了仪表板的混乱，并使多源仪表板更易于创作和维护。

自定义和查询变量类型支持此功能。对于查询变量，您可以为 Infinity 和 PostgreSQL 数据源创建多属性变量。

您可以在我们的 文档。

改进的仪表可视化

在所有版本的公共预览中提供Grafana

我们重新构想了仪表可视化，以根据您的需求提供更多数据表示选项。

例如，引入了圆形形状作为现有形状的替代方案新的样式选项下的Arc仪表。

我们还向仪表添加了迷你图支持，这提高了其作为时间序列数据统计可视化替代方案的实用性。

我们添加了梯度支持不仅仅是看起来漂亮；它可以帮助用户解释具有阈值的面板或使用连续配色方案的系列。

新的仪表可视化还具有可访问性改进。中心发光选项将仪表的当前颜色值渲染为仪表文本后面的透明发光。这取代了以前更改文本本身颜色的行为，并有助于避免文本因单个用户的主题而变得难以辨认的情况。

最后，还有其他几个新的配置选项，包括：

  • 圆角条（在条形样式下）
  • 可配置的条粗细
  • 端点标记以提高可读性
  • 新的文本显示模式可精确配置文本中显示的信息。可视化

要尝试改进的仪表可视化，请启用newGauge功能切换。要了解有关此可视化类型的更多信息，一般情况下，请查看我们的文档。

时间范围平移和缩放

通常在所有版本中提供Grafana

我们很高兴在 Grafana 仪表板和面板中引入时间范围平移和缩放，这是一种更直观、更高效地探索指标和数据的新方法。 

您可以使用仪表板级控件来向后或向前跳转一个完整的时间跨度。为了更精确的时间旅行，我们还使时间序列可视化的 x 轴在面板级别上具有交互性。只需单击并水平拖动 x 轴时间戳即可在时间上向前或向后移动，然后松开即可更新仪表板时间范围。

要放大时间范围，请在可视化区域中单击并从开始时间拖动到任意结束时间，然后松开。要缩小，请双击可视化区域，将中心两侧的时间范围扩大一半，显示两倍的时间。

最后，Grafana 中添加了两个新的时间范围键盘快捷键。输入 t+ 会放大到当前时间范围的一半，相反 t- 会缩小到当前时间范围的两倍。

时间范围平移和缩放适用于所有支持时间序列数据的可视化效果：

  • 烛台
  • 热图
  • 状态时间线
  • 状态历史记录
  • 时间系列

更高效、灵活的日志探索

Grafana 12.4 提供了新的、更可自定义的选项来探索日志数据，特别是对于使用 OpenTelemetry 或 Grafana Logs Drilldown 无查询应用程序的团队。

增强了 OpenTelemetry 日志行的显示

在所有版本中进行实验Grafana

Grafana 现在提供更智能的默认值来浏览 OpenTelemetry 日志行，并针对 OT 方式进行了优化el 结构日志数据。

OpenTelemetry 日志生成器在日志正文中发送较少的信息，并以结构化元数据或资源属性、日志字段和日志属性的键值对形式发送更多数据。以前，大部分信息仅在展开日志行时可见，因此很容易错过重要的上下文。

现在，Grafana 检测 OTel 日志行并直接在日志视图中显示相关元数据。总体而言，此更改使 OpenTelemetry 日志更易于扫描、更快理解并且默认情况下更有用，特别是在高基数或高度结构化的日志记录环境中。 

如需了解更多信息，请参阅我们的新增功能页面。 

日志深入更新

在所有版本的 Grafana 中提供公共预览版

通过 Grafana 日志深入分析，您可以可视化和探索日志，而无需编写 LogQL 查询。作为我们用于无查询、点击式数据探索的 Grafana Drilldown 应用程序套件的一部分，Logs Drilldown 现在允许您：

  • 配置默认列：您现在可以直接从插件配置页面为日志深入配置默认列。这使管理员可以根据选定的标签集定义默认显示哪些字段（或列），确保用户立即看到与其用例最相关的信息。
  • 保存并恢复日志探索：您现在可以在日志深入分析中保存日志探索并稍后返回。保存的探索包括您的活动过滤器，因此您不必在每次调查问题时重新创建相同的视图。

请查看我们的技术文档以了解有关日志的更多信息深入了解。

身份和访问管理增强功能

以下是一些最新功能，可帮助您保护 Grafana 环境并管理用户访问。 

SCIM 用户和团队配置

通常在 Grafana Cloud 和 Grafana Enterprise 中提供

您现在可以直接从身份提供商 (IdP) 自动化整个用户生命周期和团队结构。 

我们去年在公共预览版中引入了 SCIM，随着 Grafana 12.4 的一部分转向全面可用，我们已经消除了“首次登录”瓶颈。这使得中心化运营商能够在用户打开应用程序之前对其进行愿景、配置和保护。

您现在可以在几分钟内构建、测试和扩展整个工程组织结构，而无需管理复杂的同步管道并等待用户登录来分配权限。主要优势包括：

  • 立即加入：用户和团队在添加到您的 IdP 时立即创建，从而可以在 IRM 等工具中立即进行配置。
  • 自动取消配置：在 IdP 中停用用户后，通过自动席位回收和即时访问删除让您高枕无忧。
  • 更快地扩展：SCIM 的早期采用者看到与仅使用 SAML 的客户相比，90 天内的活跃用户数更高，因为消除手动摩擦可加速平台采用。

要了解更多信息，请查看我们的文档和这篇博文。 

针对已保存查询的 RBAC

在 Grafana Cloud 和 Grafana Enterprise 中提供公共预览版

已保存查询 - 中的一项功能Grafana 可以轻松保存、共享和重用查询 - 现在支持基于角色的访问控制 (RBAC)，为团队提供了更清晰、更安全的方式来共享和管理查询。

两个提供自定义角色：

  • 写入者：创建、更新和删除已保存的查询
  • 读取者：重用现有已保存的查询

这用一致的、基于角色的权限取代了之前的每个查询共享复选框，使您可以更轻松地协作处理已保存的查询，而无需担心意外更改的风险。

要了解更多信息，请查看我们已保存的查询文档。

扩展 Grafana：数据源更新

我们相信您应该能够查询和可视化您的数据，无论数据位于何处。因此，我们始终致力于扩展和增强 Grafana 数据源阵容。 

以下是最新更新。

Grafana 的 Zabbix 数据源的新版本

在 Grafana 的所有版本中普遍可用

Grafana 的 Zabbix 数据源在这里，提供更广泛的共享功能和更高的稳定性，以实现顺利进行

主要更新包括：

  • 与任何人共享仪表板：您现在可以在外部共享 Zabbix 的 Grafana 仪表板，而无需收件人有权访问您的 Grafana 组织。 
  • 增强稳定性和性能：新的查询护栏在查询可能超出 Zabbix API 限制时提供主动警告，帮助您防止性能下降和意外的资源使用。您还可以在配置中显式设置查询超时，防止长时间运行的查询影响您的系统。
  • 使用主机标签进行精细过滤：您现在可以直接在 Grafana Zabbix 查询中使用 Zabbix 主机标签，从而实现更精细、灵活的数据过滤和可视化。

您可以在我们的文档。 

Google 表格数据源中的默认电子表格

在所有版本的 Grafana 中普遍可用

您现在可以创建 Google 表格数据源时设置默认电子表格，以便在使用数据源时自动可用。这意味着您可以定义一次并在仪表板、面板和查询中重复使用它，而不是重复选择同一文件。

结果是为常见用例（例如在共享电子表格中跟踪 KPI、可视化操作清单或根据协作维护的数据构建内部仪表板）提供更顺畅的工作流程。 

要详细了解 Grafana 的 Google 表格数据源，请访问我们的文档。 

了解有关 Grafana 的更多信息

有关 Grafana 中所有新功能的详细列表，请查看我们的 Grafana 文档，即 Grafana 变更日志，或我们的新增文档。

加入 Grafana 实验室社区

我们邀请您参与 Grafana Labs 社区论坛。分享您使用新功能的体验，讨论最佳实践，并探索将这些更新集成到您的工作流程中的创造性方法。您的见解和用例对于丰富 Grafana 生态系统非常宝贵。

升级到 Grafana 12.4

下载 Grafana 12.4 tod您可以注册 Grafana Cloud 来体验所有最新功能，它为每个用例提供了真正有用的永久免费套餐和计划。立即注册免费 Grafana Cloud 帐户。

我们的Grafana 升级指南还提供为那些希望从早期版本升级以确保顺利过渡的用户提供分步说明。

特别感谢我们的社区

我们向 Grafana 表示衷心的感谢社区！

您的贡献（从拉取请求到有价值的反馈）对于不断增强 Grafana 至关重要。您的热情和奉献精神激励我们 Grafana Labs 不断创新和提升 Grafana 平台。

Grafana Cloud 是开始使用指标、日志、跟踪、仪表板等的最简单方法。我们为每个用例提供慷慨的永久免费套餐和计划。 立即免费注册！

item.title: 【The Grafana Cloud identity blueprint: balancing security and scale】Grafana Cloud 身份蓝图：平衡安全性和规模
item.description:
【

If you've ever rolled out Grafana Cloud to a growing engineering organization, this pattern may sound familiar:

Everything feels simple at first. You invite a few teammates, give them access, and dashboards start appearing. Then the team grows. Then the number of stacks grows. Over time, a model that once felt fast and empowering starts to feel risky, difficult to understand, and even harder to undo.

This post is about avoiding that moment.

In a previous post, I described the single-stack model, where Grafana teams and folders handle multi-tenancy inside a single Grafana stack. That model scales well operationally, but only if your identity and access approach scales alongside it.

As organizations grow, a new question tends to surface: How do I give engineers fast and automated access to Grafana without turning my entire Grafana Cloud account into a shared administrative surface?

The answer is not more manual role assignments or more process. Instead, it is a layered identity model, with SCIM acting as the mechanism that allows that model to work reliably at scale.

The starting point: standard Grafana Cloud access

Most teams begin with the default Grafana Cloud access model.

Users are added directly in the Grafana Cloud portal and assigned an organization role such as admin, editor, or viewer. That role is automatically inherited as a basic role by every Grafana stack in the account.

Authentication is intentionally simple. Users can sign in with a username and password or with a social login provider such as Google or GitHub. For small teams, this simplicity is a major advantage. You can be productive almost immediately, without investing time in identity integration.

At the same time, this model relies on an assumption that does not hold indefinitely. It assumes that anyone who needs Grafana access should also have account-wide visibility and permissions.

As usage grows, that assumption becomes difficult to defend. Universal access is harder to audit, harder to govern, and riskier to revoke cleanly. The solution is not to abandon simplicity, but to separate where Grafana is administered from where it is actually used.

Why SCIM matters for operating at scale

For organizations using Okta or Entra ID, SCIM provisioning is what makes a layered identity model practical.

Rather than manually creating and maintaining teams, SCIM allows your identity provider to push users and teams into Grafana automatically, with membership already defined. Your IdP becomes the system of record for who exists and where they belong.

SCIM also enables immediate deprovisioning. When a user is disabled in your identity provider, their access to the Grafana stack is removed in near real-time. This significantly reduces exposure from stale accounts and offboarding gaps.

Just as importantly, SCIM enables day-zero readiness when combined with RBAC, folders, and data source permissions. New engineers arrive to find their dashboards, folders, and team memberships already in place, without manual setup or access requests.

As organizations grow, manual access models do not merely slow things down. They eventually become a source of operational and security risk. With SCIM now generally available for Grafana Cloud stacks, this approach is accessible well before teams reach that breaking point.

Note: SCIM provisioning uses credentials (a token or service account) to automate provisioning. Treat it like an admin credential: store it in a vault/secret manager, rotate it regularly, restrict who can create/manage it, and monitor provisioning activity for anomalies.

Layer one: the Grafana Cloud portal as an administrative vault

The Grafana Cloud portal acts as the control plane for your account. It governs billing, stack creation, support access, and account-level configuration. Because it represents your entire observability footprint, it benefits from being treated as a tightly controlled administrative space.

In practice, this means that access to the portal should be limited to a small group of platform or observability administrators. Most engineers don’t need to sign in to grafana.com at all. Keeping this group small reduces the potential blast radius of mistakes and simplifies compliance and auditing.

Access to the portal is best managed declaratively or through your identity provider. Terraform-based management and private SSO both ensure that authentication strength and lifecycle policies live in your IdP rather than being managed manually in Grafana.

It is also a good practice to maintain a single "break glass" account. This account should not rely on SSO and should be stored securely in a password vault with access reviewed and tested periodically. Its purpose is to ensure you retain access if your identity provider is unavailable.

Bottom line: This layer is intentionally restrictive. The Grafana Cloud portal is designed for operators, not for day-to-day engineering work.

Layer two: the Grafana stack as an automated workspace

The Grafana stack is where engineering teams spend their time. This is where dashboards are explored, alerts are investigated, and incidents are understood.

At this layer, the priority shifts from strict control to predictable automation.

In more security-conscious environments, engineers should authenticate directly against their stack URL, such as your-org.grafana.net. Stack-level SSO reduces the risk that the portal becomes an unintended access path to production data.

User and team creation at the stack level should be driven by your identity provider rather than by manual invitation. Engineers should not have to wait for access to be granted. Ideally, access already exists before they ever sign in.

Even outside of Okta or Entra ID environments, stack-level private SSO using SAML, OIDC, or OAuth allows Grafana to defer authentication decisions to the system you already trust.

This separation is what allows tight governance at the account level while preserving a smooth experience for engineers.

As organizations grow, manual access isn't just slow, it can become a security risk. Using SCIM for Grafana stacks (which is now generally available for Okta and Entra ID) lets you automate the boring parts of access control before things get messy.

Once it’s running, you should set up alerts for your provisioning logs to flag anything unusual, such as:

  • Unexpected spikes in user creation.
  • Bulk deletes or disables that weren't planned.
  • Changes to team memberships in sensitive groups.

You can find the technical details in our SCIM documentation, or speak to your account team if you want to try the Private Preview for Grafana Cloud portal SSO.

Grafana Cloud is the easiest way to get started with metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, and more. We have a generous forever-free tier and plans for every use case. Sign up for free now!

】

如果您曾经向不断发展的工程组织部署过 Grafana Cloud，这种模式可能听起来很熟悉：

一开始一切都感觉很简单。您邀请一些队友，授予他们访问权限，然后仪表板开始出现。然后团队就会成长。然后堆栈的数量就会增加。随着时间的推移，曾经感觉快速且强大的模型开始变得有风险、难以理解，甚至更难撤销。

这篇文章就是为了避免那个时刻。

在 上一篇中帖子中，我描述了单堆栈模型，其中 Grafana 团队和文件夹在单个 Grafana 堆栈内处理多租户。该模型在操作上可以很好地扩展，但前提是您的身份和访问方法能够随之扩展。

随着组织的发展，一个新问题往往会浮出水面：如何让工程师快速、自动地访问 Grafana，而不将整个 Grafana Cloud 帐户转变为共享管理界面？

答案不是更多的手动角色分配或更多流程。相反，它是一个分层身份模型，SCIM 充当允许该模型大规模可靠工作的机制。

起点：标准 Grafana Cloud 访问

大多数团队从默认的 Grafana Cloud 访问模型开始。

用户直接添加到Grafana Cloud 门户并分配了组织角色例如管理员、编辑者或查看者。该角色会自动被系统中的每个 Grafana 堆栈继承为基本角色帐户。

身份验证故意简单。用户可以使用用户名和密码登录，也可以使用 Google 或 GitHub 等社交登录提供商登录。对于小型团队来说，这种简单性是一个主要优势。您几乎可以立即提高工作效率，而无需在身份集成上投入时间。

同时，该模型依赖于一个不能无限期保持的假设。它假设任何需要 Grafana 访问权限的人都应该拥有帐户范围的可见性和权限。

随着使用量的增长，这一假设变得难以辩护。普遍访问更难审计、更难管理，而且彻底撤销的风险更大。解决方案不是放弃简单性，而是将 Grafana 的管理位置与实际使用位置分开。

为什么 SCIM 对操作很重要大规模部署

对于使用 Okta 或 Entra ID 的组织来说，SCIM 配置使分层身份模型变得实用。

SCIM 不是手动创建和维护团队，而是允许您的身份提供商将用户和团队自动推送到 Grafana，并已定义成员资格。您的 IdP 成为记录谁存在及其所属位置的系统。

SCIM 还可以立即取消配置。当用户在您的身份提供商中被禁用时，他们对 Grafana 堆栈的访问权限将被近乎实时地删除。这显着减少了陈旧帐户和离职间隙带来的风险。

同样重要的是，SCIM 与 RBAC、文件夹和数据源权限。新工程师到达时会发现他们的仪表板、文件夹和团队成员身份已就位，无需手动设置或访问请求。

随着组织的发展，手动访问模型不仅会减慢速度。它们最终成为运营和安全风险的来源。由于 SCIM 现在普遍可用于 Grafana Cloud 堆栈，因此在团队达到突破点之前就可以使用这种方法。

注意：SCIM 配置使用凭据（令牌或服务帐户）来自动配置。将其视为管理员凭据：将其存储在保管库/秘密管理器中，定期轮换，限制谁可以创建/管理它，并监控异常的配置活动。

第一层：Grafana Cloud 门户作为管理保管库

Grafana Cloud 门户充当您帐户的控制平面。它管理计费、堆栈创建、支持访问和帐户级配置。因为它代表了您的整个可观察性足迹，所以它受益于被视为严格控制的管理空间。

实际上，这意味着对门户的访问应仅限于一小部分平台或可观察性管理员。大多数工程师根本不需要登录 grafana.com。保持该组规模较小可以减少错误的潜在影响范围，并简化合规性和审核。

最好以声明方式或通过您的身份提供商来管理对门户的访问。基于 Terraform 的管理和私有 SSO 都确保身份验证强度和生命周期策略存在于您的 IdP 中，而不是在 Grafana 中手动管理。

维护单个“bre”也是一个很好的做法。ak glass”帐户。此帐户不应依赖于 SSO，并且应安全地存储在密码库中，并定期审查和测试访问权限。其目的是确保您在身份提供商不可用时保留访问权限。

底线：这一层有意进行限制。Grafana Cloud 门户是为操作员设计的，而不是为日常工程工作而设计。

第二层：Grafana 堆栈作为自动化工作区

Grafana 堆栈这是工程团队花费时间的地方。这是探索仪表板、调查警报和理解事件的地方。

在这一层，优先级从严格控制转向可预测的自动化。

在更加注重安全的环境中，工程师应直接针对其堆栈 URL 进行身份验证，例如 your-org.grafana.net。 href="https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/setup-grafana/configure-access/configure-authentication/?pg=blog&plcmt=body-txt">堆栈级 SSO 降低了门户成为生产数据意外访问路径的风险。

堆栈级别的用户和团队创建应由身份提供商驱动，而不是由手动邀请驱动。理想情况下，访问权限在他们登录之前就已经存在。

即使在 Okta 或 Entra ID 环境之外，使用 SAML、OIDC 或 OAuth 的堆栈级私有 SSO 也允许 Grafana 将身份验证决策推迟到您已经信任的系统。

这种分离允许在帐户级别进行严格管理，同时为工程师提供流畅的体验。

随着组织的发展，手动访问不仅速度缓慢，而且使用 SCIM 可能会成为安全风险。 Grafana 堆栈（现已普遍适用于 Okta 和 Entra ID）可让您在事情变得混乱之前自动执行访问控制的无聊部分。

运行后，您应该为配置日志设置警报以标记任何异常情况，例如：

  • 用户创建中出现意外峰值。
  • 批量删除或禁用未计划的内容。
  • 更改团队成员资格敏感群体。

您可以在我们的SCIM文档中找到技术详细信息，或者如果您想尝试 Grafana Cloud 门户的私有预览版，请与您的客户团队联系SSO。

Grafana Cloud是开始使用指标、日志、跟踪、仪表板等的最简单方法。我们为每个用例提供了慷慨的永久免费套餐和计划。 href="https://grafana.com/auth/sign-up/create-user/?pg=blog&plcmt=body-txt">立即免费注册！
